## Forum Threads [...] Promoted

Gary Miklos

NoMac.app

NoMac.app

Maker

📌

Me and my friends moved our whole AI agent setup from my Mac to Hetzner. But we still kept getting stuck on the MacBook whenever working on native iOS apps. We didn’t want to pay $100+/month for a cloud Mac, and Mac Minis were out of stock. So we built this: NoMac.app - let your AI agents build and publish native iOS apps without a Mac.

Report

Chalermpon Ananwattanakit

Does the App Store rejection feedback make it back to the agent through the MCP, or is that where a human takes over? Review is the one step in the pipeline that stays human on Apple's side, so I wondered where the loop closes. Congrats on the launch, Gary.

Report

Gary Miklos

NoMac.app

NoMac.app

Gary Miklos

NoMac.app

NoMac.app

Maker

@vollos Yes, the agent can poll the the submission status and get back the whole feedback, so ready to act without human action!

Report

Chalermpon Ananwattanakit

@gary\_miklos Nice, so the loop does close. An agent that reads Apple's rejection notes and ships the fix itself, didn't expect that to work already. Hope the numbers keep climbing today.

Report

Matt Johnson

cool idea, I was thinking of launching an iOS app but having to get Xcode and a newer mac to run it was annoying. How is using this better than me just asking claude code to make it for me on a PC?

Report

# NoMac.appAlternatives

Exploring options beyond NoMac.app? Try Xcode, Apple’s native IDE for full simulator and device testing. Automate builds, signing, and uploads with fastlane, or chat your way to prototypes using Rork. Need instant cloud deploys? Codesphere spins up environments in seconds for agent-driven pipelines.
